# Journal: 2026-04-13

## Summary

Started campaign list pagination implementation. Set up React Query infrastructure and designed cursor schema with backend team.

## Work Done

- Reviewed existing CampaignListPage component (apps/nestads-frontend/src/pages/campaigns/index.tsx).
- Identified that current implementation fetches all campaigns via useCampaigns() with no pagination.
- Met with backend team to finalize /v2/campaigns cursor schema.
- Agreed: cursor = base64(lastUpdatedAt + ":" + lastId), opaque to frontend.
- Set up queryKeyFactory for campaigns: ['campaigns', { status, dateFrom, dateTo, projectId, cursor }].
- Implemented useCampaignList hook skeleton with React Query useInfiniteQuery.
- Tested cursor encode/decode with sample data — works correctly.
- Added URL search params sync via useSearchParams for filter state.
- Verified filter params round-trip through URL encoding without loss.
- Created CampaignFilterPanel component shell with status dropdown.

## Decisions

- Use useInfiniteQuery instead of useQuery for cursor pagination (better UX for load-more pattern).
- Store only current page cursor in URL, not full cursor history (back button uses browser history).
- Status enum values: ACTIVE, PAUSED, DRAFT, ARCHIVED (matches backend API contract).

## Blockers

- /v2/campaigns returns 404 in staging — backend WP-4315 not yet deployed.
- Figma spec incomplete — design review scheduled for 2026-04-14.

## Next

- Build date range picker component.
- Add project filter dropdown with search.
- Implement skeleton loader component.
- Write unit tests for useCampaignList hook.
- Write unit tests for URL sync logic.
- Check in with @ux-lead on Figma spec status.
- Review React Query devtools integration with existing app setup.
- Investigate scroll position preservation options (native browser API vs manual).

## Notes

- Cursor decode for debug: atob(cursor).split(':') gives [timestamp, id].
- React Query cache TTL set to 30s for campaign list (matches expected update frequency).
